dialogues.english: Okay, everyone, this is it! Let's bring the house down!
dialogues.japanese: さあ、みんな、これだ！会場を沸かせよう！

idioms.meaning: To make an audience react with great enthusiasm, especially by applauding
idioms.meaning_ja: 観客を大いに沸かせる、大喝采を浴びる

dialogues.english: I have butterflies in my stomach every single time.
dialogues.japanese: 毎回、緊張でお腹がドキドキするわ。

idioms.meaning: A nervous feeling in the stomach, usually before something important
idioms.meaning_ja: 緊張して胸がドキドキする、不安で落ち着かない

dialogues.english: Do not worry, Rosita! We were born to do this!
dialogues.japanese: 心配しないで、ロジータ！僕たちはこのために生まれてきたんだ！

idioms.meaning: Naturally talented or destined for something
idioms.meaning_ja: このために生まれてきた、天職である

dialogues.english: I've been working on this number day and night. I really hope it pays off.
dialogues.japanese: この曲を昼も夜も練習してきたんだ。報われるといいな。

idioms.meaning: All the time, without stopping
idioms.meaning_ja: 昼も夜も、四六時中

idioms.meaning: To yield good results after effort or investment
idioms.meaning_ja: 努力が報われる、成果が出る

dialogues.english: You guys are going to be great. Just go out there and give it your all.
dialogues.japanese: みんな最高になるよ。全力を出し切って。

idioms.meaning: To put in maximum effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 全力を尽くす、持てる力をすべて出す

dialogues.english: I just hope I don't freeze up on stage again.
dialogues.japanese: また舞台で固まらないといいけど。

idioms.meaning: To suddenly become unable to move or speak due to fear or nervousness
idioms.meaning_ja: 緊張で固まる、動けなくなる

dialogues.english: Meena, you have nothing to worry about. You always come through when it counts.
dialogues.japanese: ミーナ、心配することないよ。君はいつも肝心な時にやり遂げる。

idioms.meaning: To succeed or deliver results when needed
idioms.meaning_ja: やり遂げる、期待に応える

idioms.meaning: At the most important moment
idioms.meaning_ja: 肝心な時に、大事な場面で

dialogues.english: Mr. Moon! The curtain goes up in five minutes!
dialogues.japanese: ムーンさん！開演まであと5分です！

idioms.meaning: The show is about to begin
idioms.meaning_ja: 幕が上がる、ショーが始まる

dialogues.english: All right, places everyone! It's showtime! Break a leg!
dialogues.japanese: よし、みんな配置について！ショーの時間だ！成功を祈る！

idioms.meaning: A way to wish someone good luck, especially before a performance
idioms.meaning_ja: （舞台の前に）成功を祈る、頑張って

idioms.meaning: The moment when something important is about to happen
idioms.meaning_ja: 本番の時間、いよいよ始まる

dialogues.english: Let's knock 'em dead!
dialogues.japanese: みんなを圧倒しよう！

idioms.meaning: To greatly impress an audience
idioms.meaning_ja: 観客を圧倒する、大成功させる

dialogues.english: The show was a huge hit. We had them eating out of our hands.
dialogues.japanese: ショーは大成功だった。観客を虜にしたよ。

idioms.meaning: A great success
idioms.meaning_ja: 大成功、大ヒット

idioms.meaning: Completely under our influence or control; very eager to please
idioms.meaning_ja: 手のひらの上で転がす、完全に魅了する

dialogues.english: Not bad, Mr. Moon. But you still have a long way to go.
dialogues.japanese: 悪くないわ、ムーンさん。でもまだまだ先は長いわよ。

idioms.meaning: Much more progress or improvement is needed
idioms.meaning_ja: まだまだ先は長い、道のりは遠い

dialogues.english: I want to take our show to the next level. We need to think big!
dialogues.japanese: 僕たちのショーをもう一段上に持っていきたい。大きく考えなきゃ！

idioms.expression: take it to the next level
idioms.meaning: To improve or advance something significantly
idioms.meaning_ja: 次のレベルに引き上げる、大きく飛躍させる

idioms.meaning: To have ambitious plans or ideas
idioms.meaning_ja: 大きく考える、野心的な目標を持つ

dialogues.english: Buster, are you out of your mind? Redshore City is the big leagues!
dialogues.japanese: バスター、正気か？レッドショア・シティは大舞台だぞ！

idioms.meaning: Crazy, not thinking clearly
idioms.meaning_ja: 正気じゃない、おかしくなった

idioms.meaning: The highest level of competition or achievement
idioms.meaning_ja: 大舞台、一流の世界

dialogues.english: Exactly! That's where we belong. We have to shoot for the stars.
dialogues.japanese: その通り！そこが僕たちの居場所だ。星を目指さなきゃ。

idioms.meaning: To aim for the highest possible goal
idioms.meaning_ja: 高い目標を目指す、星を射る

dialogues.english: But what about our families? We can't just up and leave.
dialogues.japanese: でも家族はどうするの？急に出て行くわけにはいかないわ。

idioms.meaning: To suddenly depart without much planning
idioms.meaning_ja: 急に出て行く、突然いなくなる

dialogues.english: Come on, Rosita! This is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity! You can't pass it up!
dialogues.japanese: ロジータ、行こうよ！一生に一度のチャンスだよ！見逃しちゃダメだ！

idioms.meaning: So special that it is unlikely to happen again
idioms.meaning_ja: 一生に一度の、めったにない

idioms.meaning: To let an opportunity go by without taking it
idioms.meaning_ja: 見逃す、チャンスを逃す

dialogues.english: I'm in. I've been itching to prove myself on a bigger stage.
dialogues.japanese: 僕は行くよ。もっと大きな舞台で自分を証明したくてうずうずしてたんだ。

idioms.meaning: Very eager or impatient to do something
idioms.meaning_ja: 〜したくてうずうずしている、待ちきれない

idioms.meaning: To demonstrate one's ability or worth
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分の実力を証明する

dialogues.english: Count me in. I could use a change of scenery.
dialogues.japanese: 私も参加するわ。気分転換が必要だったの。

idioms.meaning: Include me; I want to participate
idioms.meaning_ja: 私も入れて、参加するよ

idioms.meaning: A move to a different environment or surroundings
idioms.meaning_ja: 気分転換、環境を変えること

dialogues.english: I guess I'm on board too. No turning back now.
dialogues.japanese: 私も賛成するわ。もう後には引けないね。

idioms.meaning: In agreement; willing to participate
idioms.meaning_ja: 賛成して、参加する気持ちで

idioms.meaning: It is too late to change the decision or direction
idioms.meaning_ja: もう後戻りはできない

dialogues.english: This is our shot. We have to make a lasting impression.
dialogues.japanese: これが僕たちのチャンスだ。強烈な印象を残さなきゃ。

idioms.meaning: Our chance or opportunity
idioms.meaning_ja: 私たちのチャンス、好機

idioms.meaning: To create a memorable effect on someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 強い印象を残す、忘れられない印象を与える

dialogues.english: Mr. Crystal is a tough nut to crack. He turns down almost everyone.
dialogues.japanese: クリスタル氏は手強いわよ。ほとんど全員を断るの。

idioms.meaning: A person or problem that is very difficult to deal with
idioms.meaning_ja: 手強い相手、難しい問題

idioms.meaning: To reject or refuse
idioms.meaning_ja: 断る、却下する

dialogues.english: Don't worry. We'll win him over with our talent.
dialogues.japanese: 心配しないで。才能で彼を説得してみせるよ。

idioms.meaning: To persuade someone to support you or agree with you
idioms.meaning_ja: 説得する、味方につける

dialogues.english: So, you're the little theater troupe from the sticks. Let's see what you've got.
dialogues.japanese: それで、君たちが田舎の小さな劇団か。何ができるか見せてもらおう。

idioms.meaning: From a rural or unsophisticated area
idioms.meaning_ja: 田舎から来た、地方出身の

idioms.meaning: What abilities or skills you have
idioms.meaning_ja: 何ができるか、実力を

dialogues.english: Thank you, Mr. Crystal. We've put our hearts and souls into this performance.
dialogues.japanese: ありがとうございます、クリスタルさん。この公演に心血を注いできました。

idioms.expression: put our hearts and souls into
idioms.meaning: To devote all of one's energy and passion to something
idioms.meaning_ja: 心血を注ぐ、全身全霊を込める

dialogues.english: Hmm. Not bad, but it's nothing to write home about. I've seen better.
dialogues.japanese: ふむ。悪くないが、特筆すべきことはない。もっといいものを見てきた。

idioms.meaning: Not especially remarkable or exciting
idioms.meaning_ja: 特筆すべきことはない、たいしたことない

dialogues.english: Wait! What if I told you we could get Clay Calloway to be in our show?
dialogues.japanese: 待ってください！クレイ・キャロウェイをショーに出せると言ったら？

dialogues.english: Clay Calloway? Now you're talking! That changes the whole ball game.
dialogues.japanese: クレイ・キャロウェイ？それは話が違う！状況が完全に変わるな。

idioms.meaning: Used to express enthusiasm when someone says something agreeable
idioms.meaning_ja: それでこそだ、いいぞ

idioms.meaning: Completely changes the situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 状況を一変させる、ゲームチェンジャーだ

dialogues.english: Buster! What are you doing? You're getting in over your head!
dialogues.japanese: バスター！何してるの？自分の手に負えないことを始めてるわよ！

idioms.meaning: Involved in something too difficult to handle
idioms.meaning_ja: 手に負えない状況に入り込む、能力を超えた

dialogues.english: Trust me. I'll figure it out. I always land on my feet.
dialogues.japanese: 信じて。なんとかするよ。僕はいつもうまく切り抜けるんだ。

idioms.meaning: To find a solution to a problem
idioms.meaning_ja: なんとかする、解決策を見つける

idioms.meaning: To recover well from a difficult situation
idioms.meaning_ja: うまく切り抜ける、困難からうまく立ち直る

dialogues.english: You've got yourself a deal, Moon. But don't let me down.
dialogues.japanese: 契約成立だ、ムーン。だが失望させるなよ。

idioms.meaning: I agree to the arrangement
idioms.meaning_ja: 契約成立だ、取引に合意する

idioms.meaning: To disappoint someone by failing to do what they expected
idioms.meaning_ja: 失望させる、がっかりさせる

dialogues.english: You won't regret it, sir. We'll blow your mind.
dialogues.japanese: 後悔させません。度肝を抜いてみせます。

idioms.meaning: To amaze or shock someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 度肝を抜く、驚かせる

dialogues.english: Buster, you really stuck your neck out back there. Do you even know Clay Calloway?
dialogues.japanese: バスター、さっきは本当に首を突っ込んだな。クレイ・キャロウェイを知ってるのか？

idioms.meaning: To take a risk or put oneself in a vulnerable position
idioms.meaning_ja: 危険を冒す、首を突っ込む

dialogues.english: Not exactly. But we'll cross that bridge when we come to it.
dialogues.japanese: 正確には知らないけど。その時が来たら考えよう。

idioms.expression: cross that bridge when we come to it
idioms.meaning: To deal with a problem only when it actually happens
idioms.meaning_ja: その時が来たら考える、先のことは後で考える

dialogues.english: Wow! Look at this place! It's out of this world!
dialogues.japanese: わあ！この場所を見て！この世のものとは思えない！

idioms.meaning: Extremely impressive or enjoyable
idioms.meaning_ja: この世のものとは思えない、素晴らしい

dialogues.english: It's pretty flashy. Not really my cup of tea, but I can see the appeal.
dialogues.japanese: かなり派手ね。私の好みじゃないけど、魅力は分かるわ。

idioms.meaning: Not something one likes or is interested in
idioms.meaning_ja: 好みではない、趣味に合わない

dialogues.english: This is the real deal. We're playing with the big boys now.
dialogues.japanese: これが本物だ。一流の人たちと一緒にやるんだ。

idioms.meaning: Something authentic or genuinely impressive
idioms.meaning_ja: 本物、正真正銘の

idioms.meaning: Competing at the highest level
idioms.meaning_ja: 一流の人たちと張り合う、本格的な世界に入る

dialogues.english: I just hope we're not biting off more than we can chew.
dialogues.japanese: 身の丈に合わないことをしていなければいいけど。

idioms.expression: biting off more than we can chew
idioms.meaning: Trying to do more than one is able to handle
idioms.meaning_ja: 身の丈に合わないことをする、手を広げすぎる

dialogues.english: Everything here is so over the top. I feel like a fish out of water.
dialogues.japanese: ここは何もかも派手すぎる。場違いな気分だわ。

idioms.meaning: Excessively elaborate or extravagant
idioms.meaning_ja: 度を越した、大げさすぎる

idioms.meaning: Someone who feels uncomfortable in unfamiliar surroundings
idioms.meaning_ja: 場違いな人、慣れない環境で居心地が悪い

dialogues.english: Come on, everyone! This is our moment in the spotlight. Let's make the most of it!
dialogues.japanese: みんな、行こう！これが僕たちの晴れ舞台だ。最大限に活かそう！

idioms.meaning: A time of public attention or fame
idioms.meaning_ja: 注目を浴びる時、晴れ舞台

idioms.meaning: To use an opportunity to the fullest advantage
idioms.meaning_ja: 最大限に活かす、精一杯利用する

dialogues.english: I have this amazing idea for the show! It came to me out of the blue!
dialogues.japanese: ショーの素晴らしいアイデアがあるんだ！突然ひらめいたんだ！

idioms.meaning: Unexpectedly, without any warning
idioms.meaning_ja: 突然、思いがけなく

dialogues.english: Okay, Gunter, I'm all ears. What's this big idea?
dialogues.japanese: わかった、ガンター、聞いてるよ。その大きなアイデアって何？

idioms.meaning: Listening with full attention
idioms.meaning_ja: 全神経を集中して聞いている、洗いざらい聞くよ

dialogues.english: A space-themed show! With aliens and rockets! It'll be off the charts!
dialogues.japanese: 宇宙テーマのショー！エイリアンとロケットで！最高にすごいものになるよ！

idioms.meaning: Exceeding all expectations; extraordinary
idioms.meaning_ja: 想像を超えた、規格外の

dialogues.english: That's... actually brilliant! Gunter, you hit the nail on the head!
dialogues.japanese: それは...実は素晴らしい！ガンター、まさにその通りだ！

idioms.expression: hit the nail on the head
idioms.meaning: To describe exactly what is right; to be perfectly correct
idioms.meaning_ja: 的を射る、まさにその通り

dialogues.english: A space show? That's a bit far-fetched, don't you think?
dialogues.japanese: 宇宙のショー？ちょっと突飛じゃない？

idioms.meaning: Unlikely and hard to believe
idioms.meaning_ja: 突飛な、ありそうもない

dialogues.english: Nothing is far-fetched when you put your mind to it!
dialogues.japanese: 心を込めて取り組めば、突飛なものなんてないよ！

idioms.meaning: To concentrate and make a determined effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 心を込めて取り組む、集中して努力する

dialogues.english: I like the concept. But it needs star power. Clay Calloway or the show's off.
dialogues.japanese: コンセプトは気に入った。だがスターパワーが必要だ。クレイ・キャロウェイが出なければ中止だ。

idioms.meaning: The ability of a famous person to attract public attention
idioms.meaning_ja: スターの魅力、集客力

idioms.meaning: The show is cancelled
idioms.meaning_ja: ショーは中止だ

dialogues.english: Don't worry, Mr. Crystal. I have it all under control.
dialogues.japanese: ご心配なく、クリスタルさん。すべてコントロールできています。

idioms.meaning: Being managed successfully
idioms.meaning_ja: コントロールできている、管理下にある

dialogues.english: Buster, you're walking on thin ice with Crystal. He doesn't mess around.
dialogues.japanese: バスター、クリスタル相手に危険な橋を渡ってるわよ。彼は本気よ。

idioms.meaning: In a risky or dangerous situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 危険な橋を渡る、薄氷を踏む思い

idioms.meaning: Is very serious and does not tolerate mistakes
idioms.meaning_ja: 本気だ、冗談が通じない

dialogues.english: I know what I'm doing. We just need to stay the course.
dialogues.japanese: 自分が何をしているか分かってるよ。このまま進み続けるだけだ。

idioms.meaning: To continue with a plan despite difficulties
idioms.meaning_ja: 方針を貫く、最後までやり通す

dialogues.english: Your dancing is stiff and lifeless! You need to step up your game!
dialogues.japanese: お前のダンスは硬くて生気がない！もっとレベルを上げろ！

idioms.meaning: To improve one's performance or effort
idioms.meaning_ja: レベルを上げる、もっと頑張る

dialogues.english: I'm trying my best! Cut me some slack!
dialogues.japanese: 精一杯やってるよ！少しは大目に見てくれ！

idioms.meaning: Making the greatest possible effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 最善を尽くしている、精一杯やっている

idioms.meaning: To be less critical or demanding
idioms.meaning_ja: 大目に見る、少し手加減する

dialogues.english: In this business, there's no room for excuses. Shape up or ship out!
dialogues.japanese: この業界では言い訳の余地はない。しっかりしろ、さもなければ出て行け！

idioms.meaning: Excuses are not acceptable
idioms.meaning_ja: 言い訳の余地がない

idioms.meaning: Improve your behavior or leave
idioms.meaning_ja: しっかりしろ、さもなければ出て行け

dialogues.english: I won't give up. I'll get the hang of it eventually.
dialogues.japanese: 諦めない。いつかはコツをつかむよ。

idioms.meaning: To stop trying
idioms.meaning_ja: 諦める、ギブアップする

idioms.meaning: To learn how to do something, especially after practice
idioms.meaning_ja: コツをつかむ、要領を得る

dialogues.english: Hey! You look like you could use a hand. I'm Nooshy.
dialogues.japanese: ねえ！手助けが必要そうだね。私はヌーシー。

idioms.meaning: Would benefit from some help
idioms.meaning_ja: 手助けが必要だ、助けがあるとありがたい

dialogues.english: Forget that guy. Dancing is all about letting loose and feeling the rhythm.
dialogues.japanese: あの人のことは忘れて。ダンスはリラックスしてリズムを感じることが全てだよ。

idioms.meaning: To relax and behave freely without restraint
idioms.meaning_ja: リラックスする、自由に振る舞う

dialogues.english: You make it look so easy. I feel like I have two left feet.
dialogues.japanese: 簡単そうに見せるね。僕は不器用すぎる気がする。

idioms.meaning: Being very clumsy, especially at dancing
idioms.meaning_ja: 不器用である、特にダンスが下手

dialogues.english: Everyone starts somewhere. You just need to go with the flow.
dialogues.japanese: 誰だって最初は初心者だよ。流れに身を任せればいいの。

idioms.meaning: To relax and accept a situation without trying to control it
idioms.meaning_ja: 流れに身を任せる、成り行きに従う

dialogues.english: You know what, you're right. I've been so caught up in trying to be perfect.
dialogues.japanese: そうだね、君の言う通りだ。完璧にしようとして夢中になりすぎてた。

idioms.meaning: So involved in something that you cannot think about anything else
idioms.meaning_ja: 〜に夢中になる、〜にとらわれる

dialogues.english: Now you're getting the hang of it! Just keep at it!
dialogues.japanese: そうそう、コツをつかんできたね！その調子で続けて！

idioms.meaning: To continue doing something with determination
idioms.meaning_ja: 続ける、粘り強くやる

dialogues.english: Meena, your role calls for a romantic scene. You need to come out of your shell.
dialogues.japanese: ミーナ、君の役にはロマンチックなシーンがある。殻を破らなきゃ。

idioms.meaning: To become less shy and more willing to socialize
idioms.meaning_ja: 殻を破る、打ち解ける

dialogues.english: A romantic scene? Oh no, I'm going to make a fool of myself.
dialogues.japanese: ロマンチックなシーン？ああ、恥をかきそう。

idioms.meaning: To do something that makes one look stupid
idioms.meaning_ja: 恥をかく、馬鹿にされる

dialogues.english: Hi, I'm Alfonso. Looks like we're going to be working side by side.
dialogues.japanese: こんにちは、僕はアルフォンソ。一緒に仕事をすることになりそうだね。

idioms.meaning: Together, close to each other
idioms.meaning_ja: 並んで、一緒に

dialogues.english: Oh... hi. I'm sorry, I get tongue-tied around new people.
dialogues.japanese: あ...こんにちは。ごめんなさい、初対面の人の前だと言葉に詰まるの。

idioms.meaning: Unable to speak because of shyness or nervousness
idioms.meaning_ja: 言葉に詰まる、うまく話せない

dialogues.english: Don't sweat it. Let's just take it one step at a time.
dialogues.japanese: 気にしないで。一歩ずつやっていこう。

idioms.meaning: Don't worry about it
idioms.meaning_ja: 気にしないで、心配しないで

idioms.meaning: To do things gradually, not rushing
idioms.meaning_ja: 一歩ずつ、焦らずに

dialogues.english: I keep second-guessing everything I do on stage.
dialogues.japanese: 舞台でやることを全部、疑ってばかりいるの。

idioms.meaning: To doubt or question a decision after it's been made
idioms.meaning_ja: 後から疑う、判断を迷う

dialogues.english: You've got talent. Sometimes you just need to take the plunge.
dialogues.japanese: 才能があるよ。時には思い切って飛び込むことも必要だ。

idioms.meaning: To commit to a course of action, especially after hesitation
idioms.meaning_ja: 思い切って飛び込む、決断する

dialogues.english: I'll try. But right now, I feel like I'm in way over my head.
dialogues.japanese: やってみるわ。でも今は、完全に手に負えない気がする。

idioms.meaning: Deeply involved in something too difficult
idioms.meaning_ja: 完全に手に負えない、能力をはるかに超えた

dialogues.english: Meena, you have what it takes. Don't sell yourself short.
dialogues.japanese: ミーナ、君には才能がある。自分を過小評価するな。

idioms.meaning: To have the qualities or abilities necessary for success
idioms.meaning_ja: 必要な才能がある、素質がある

idioms.meaning: To underestimate one's own abilities or worth
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分を過小評価する、自分を安売りする

dialogues.english: Okay. I'll give it my best shot.
dialogues.japanese: わかった。全力でやってみるわ。

idioms.meaning: To try as hard as one can
idioms.meaning_ja: 全力でやる、精一杯やってみる

dialogues.english: Ash, you're our best bet to get through to Clay Calloway. He's been off the grid for years.
dialogues.japanese: アッシュ、クレイ・キャロウェイに届くのは君が一番の頼みの綱だ。彼は何年も世間から姿を消している。

idioms.meaning: The most likely way to succeed
idioms.meaning_ja: 一番の頼み、最善の策

idioms.meaning: To succeed in communicating with or persuading someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 心に届く、説得する

idioms.meaning: Living in isolation, away from public life
idioms.meaning_ja: 世間から離れた、隠遁生活の

dialogues.english: I'll do my best, but he's a tough cookie. He shut everyone out after his wife passed away.
dialogues.japanese: 頑張るけど、彼は手強い人よ。奥さんが亡くなってからみんなを拒絶してる。

idioms.meaning: A person who is strong-willed and determined
idioms.meaning_ja: 手強い人、タフな人

idioms.meaning: To refuse to interact with others
idioms.meaning_ja: みんなを拒絶する、心を閉ざす

idioms.meaning: Died (a polite way to say someone died)
idioms.meaning_ja: 亡くなった（丁寧な表現）

dialogues.english: Mr. Calloway? I'm a huge fan. Your music really speaks to me.
dialogues.japanese: キャロウェイさん？大ファンなんです。あなたの音楽は本当に心に響きます。

idioms.meaning: Has a strong emotional impact; resonates personally
idioms.meaning_ja: 心に響く、共感する

dialogues.english: Go away. Those days are behind me. I'm done with all of that.
dialogues.japanese: 帰れ。あの日々は終わった。もう全部終わりだ。

idioms.meaning: In the past, no longer part of one's life
idioms.meaning_ja: 過去のことだ、もう終わった

idioms.meaning: Finished with; no longer willing to be involved in
idioms.meaning_ja: もう終わりだ、もう関わらない

dialogues.english: I know you've been through a lot. But your music still matters. It touched so many lives.
dialogues.japanese: たくさんの辛い経験をされたのは分かります。でもあなたの音楽はまだ大切です。多くの人の人生に触れました。

idioms.meaning: To have experienced many difficult situations
idioms.meaning_ja: たくさんの辛い経験をした

idioms.meaning: Had a positive emotional impact on many people
idioms.meaning_ja: 多くの人の人生に感動を与えた

dialogues.english: You don't know what you're talking about. Music died for me when Ruby did.
dialogues.japanese: 分かったようなことを言うな。音楽はルビーと一緒に死んだんだ。

idioms.expression: don't know what you're talking about
idioms.meaning: You don't understand the situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 分かったようなことを言うな、理解していない

dialogues.english: I wrote this song. It's inspired by your work. I just... I want you to hear it. No strings attached.
dialogues.japanese: この曲を書いたんです。あなたの作品に触発されて。ただ...聴いてほしいんです。条件なしで。

idioms.meaning: Without any conditions or obligations
idioms.meaning_ja: 条件なしで、見返りを求めずに

dialogues.english: That's... that's not half bad, kid.
dialogues.japanese: それは...悪くないな、お嬢ちゃん。

idioms.meaning: Actually quite good (understated praise)
idioms.meaning_ja: なかなかいい、意外と良い（控えめな褒め言葉）

dialogues.english: Ruby would have wanted you to keep making music. Don't let the flame die out.
dialogues.japanese: ルビーさんはあなたに音楽を続けてほしかったはず。その炎を消さないで。

idioms.meaning: To allow passion or enthusiasm to fade away
idioms.meaning_ja: 情熱の炎を消す、やる気を失わせる

dialogues.english: I'll... think about it. No promises. Now get out of here before I change my mind.
dialogues.japanese: ...考えておく。約束はしない。気が変わる前にここから出て行け。

idioms.meaning: I am not committing to anything
idioms.meaning_ja: 約束はしない、保証はしない

idioms.meaning: To alter one's decision or opinion
idioms.meaning_ja: 気が変わる、考え直す

dialogues.english: I keep falling behind in rehearsals. I feel like I'm losing my touch.
dialogues.japanese: リハーサルでどんどん遅れてる。腕が鈍ってる気がするわ。

idioms.meaning: Failing to keep up with others
idioms.meaning_ja: 遅れをとる、ついていけなくなる

idioms.meaning: Becoming less skilled at something one used to be good at
idioms.meaning_ja: 腕が鈍る、勘が鈍る

dialogues.english: Rosita, don't be so hard on yourself. You're doing great!
dialogues.japanese: ロジータ、自分に厳しくしすぎないで。すごくうまくやってるよ！

idioms.meaning: Being too critical of one's own actions
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分に厳しすぎる、自分を責めすぎる

dialogues.english: The stunts are terrifying. I'm not cut out for this kind of thing.
dialogues.japanese: スタントが怖すぎるの。私はこういうことに向いてないのよ。

idioms.meaning: Not naturally suited to a particular activity
idioms.meaning_ja: 向いていない、適性がない

dialogues.english: Rosita, I believe in you. Just hang in there a little longer.
dialogues.japanese: ロジータ、君を信じてるよ。もう少しだけ頑張って。

idioms.meaning: To have confidence in someone's abilities
idioms.meaning_ja: 君を信じている、君の力を信じている

idioms.meaning: To persist despite difficulties
idioms.meaning_ja: 頑張って、持ちこたえて

dialogues.english: Daddy says I should take over Rosita's part. She doesn't have what it takes.
dialogues.japanese: パパがロジータの役を引き継げって。彼女には才能がないって。

idioms.meaning: To assume control or responsibility for something
idioms.meaning_ja: 引き継ぐ、代わりにやる

idioms.meaning: Lacks the necessary abilities
idioms.meaning_ja: 必要な才能がない、素質がない

dialogues.english: Now wait just a minute! Rosita earned her spot fair and square.
dialogues.japanese: ちょっと待ってくれ！ロジータは正々堂々と自分の席を勝ち取ったんだ。

idioms.meaning: In an honest and straightforward manner
idioms.meaning_ja: 正々堂々と、公正に

idioms.meaning: Deserved her position through effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分の席を実力で勝ち取った

dialogues.english: My daughter gets what she wants, Moon. That's the bottom line.
dialogues.japanese: 娘は望むものを手に入れる、ムーン。それが結論だ。

idioms.meaning: The most important point or the final conclusion
idioms.meaning_ja: 最終的な結論、要するに

dialogues.english: It's okay, Buster. Maybe I should just throw in the towel.
dialogues.japanese: 大丈夫よ、バスター。もう諦めた方がいいのかも。

idioms.meaning: To admit defeat; to give up
idioms.meaning_ja: 諦める、ギブアップする、降参する

dialogues.english: No way! We're in this together. We've got each other's backs!
dialogues.japanese: ダメだよ！僕たちは一緒だ。お互いを支え合ってるんだ！

idioms.meaning: Sharing the same situation or challenge as a team
idioms.meaning_ja: 一蓮托生、一緒にやっている

idioms.meaning: Supporting and protecting each other
idioms.meaning_ja: お互いを支え合う、味方でいる

dialogues.english: You're right. I won't let them push me around.
dialogues.japanese: そうね。彼らに振り回されないわ。

idioms.meaning: To bully or dominate someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 人を振り回す、いじめる

dialogues.english: Where's Clay Calloway, Moon? I'm running out of patience.
dialogues.japanese: クレイ・キャロウェイはどこだ、ムーン？我慢の限界だぞ。

idioms.meaning: Becoming increasingly frustrated or annoyed
idioms.meaning_ja: 我慢の限界に近づいている、堪忍袋の緒が切れそう

dialogues.english: He's coming, Mr. Crystal. It's just taking a little longer than expected. These things take time.
dialogues.japanese: 来ますよ、クリスタルさん。予想より少し時間がかかっているだけです。こういうことには時間が必要なんです。

idioms.meaning: A way of saying patience is needed because progress is slow
idioms.meaning_ja: こういうことには時間がかかる

dialogues.english: Don't beat around the bush with me. Is he coming or not?
dialogues.japanese: ごまかすな。来るのか来ないのか？

idioms.meaning: To avoid talking about the main topic directly
idioms.meaning_ja: 遠回しに言う、核心に触れない

dialogues.english: I give you my word. He'll be there on opening night.
dialogues.japanese: 約束します。初日の夜に彼はそこにいます。

idioms.meaning: To promise sincerely
idioms.meaning_ja: 約束する、誓う

idioms.meaning: The first public performance of a show
idioms.meaning_ja: 初日の夜、プレミア公演

dialogues.english: For your sake, I hope so. If this falls through, you're finished in this town.
dialogues.japanese: 君のためにそう願うよ。これが失敗したら、この街では終わりだ。

idioms.meaning: For your benefit or well-being
idioms.meaning_ja: 君のために、君の身のために

idioms.meaning: To fail to happen as planned
idioms.meaning_ja: 失敗する、ダメになる

idioms.meaning: Having no future career or reputation in the area
idioms.meaning_ja: この街では終わりだ、もうやっていけない

dialogues.english: Buster, you need to level with Crystal before things get out of hand.
dialogues.japanese: バスター、事態が手に負えなくなる前にクリスタルに正直に話すべきよ。

idioms.meaning: To be honest with someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 正直に話す、本当のことを言う

idioms.meaning: To become uncontrollable
idioms.meaning_ja: 手に負えなくなる、収拾がつかなくなる

dialogues.english: I can't back down now. Too much is at stake.
dialogues.japanese: 今は引き下がれない。あまりにも多くのものがかかっている。

idioms.meaning: To withdraw from a position or confrontation
idioms.meaning_ja: 引き下がる、撤退する

idioms.meaning: At risk; in danger of being lost
idioms.meaning_ja: 危険にさらされている、かかっている

dialogues.english: This whole thing could blow up in your face, Buster.
dialogues.japanese: これは全部、裏目に出るかもしれないぞ、バスター。

idioms.meaning: To go wrong suddenly and cause problems for you
idioms.meaning_ja: 裏目に出る、自分に跳ね返ってくる

dialogues.english: Maybe. But I'd rather go down swinging than play it safe.
dialogues.japanese: かもね。でも安全策を取るより、最後まで戦って負ける方がいい。

idioms.meaning: To keep fighting until the very end, even if losing
idioms.meaning_ja: 最後まで戦う、あがきながら倒れる

idioms.meaning: To avoid taking risks
idioms.meaning_ja: 安全策を取る、無難にいく

dialogues.english: Clock's ticking, Moon. Don't drop the ball.
dialogues.japanese: 時間がないぞ、ムーン。ヘマをするな。

idioms.meaning: Time is running out
idioms.meaning_ja: 時間がない、刻一刻と迫っている

idioms.meaning: To make a mistake or fail to do something expected
idioms.meaning_ja: ヘマをする、失敗する

dialogues.english: Everyone, we need to pull ourselves together! The show is right around the corner.
dialogues.japanese: みんな、しっかりしなきゃ！ショーはもうすぐだ。

idioms.meaning: To regain composure and focus
idioms.meaning_ja: しっかりする、気を取り直す

idioms.meaning: Very near in time; about to happen soon
idioms.meaning_ja: もうすぐ、間近に迫っている

dialogues.english: Clay still hasn't gotten back to me. I'm starting to lose hope.
dialogues.japanese: クレイからまだ返事がない。希望を失いかけてる。

idioms.meaning: Replied or responded to a message
idioms.meaning_ja: 返事をくれた、折り返し連絡をくれた

idioms.meaning: To stop believing that something good will happen
idioms.meaning_ja: 希望を失う、望みがなくなる

dialogues.english: Nooshy's been helping me behind the scenes. I think I'm finally turning a corner.
dialogues.japanese: ヌーシーが裏で助けてくれてるんだ。やっと転機が来た気がする。

idioms.meaning: In secret; not visible to others
idioms.meaning_ja: 裏で、陰で、舞台裏で

idioms.meaning: Starting to improve after a difficult period
idioms.meaning_ja: 転機を迎える、好転し始める

dialogues.english: Alfonso and I are finally on the same page. The chemistry is starting to click.
dialogues.japanese: アルフォンソと私はやっと意見が一致してる。息が合い始めてきた。

idioms.meaning: In agreement; having the same understanding
idioms.meaning_ja: 意見が一致している、同じ認識を持っている

idioms.meaning: Beginning to work well together or understand something
idioms.meaning_ja: 噛み合い始める、うまくいき始める

dialogues.english: I'm the star of this show! Everyone else is just along for the ride.
dialogues.japanese: 私がこのショーのスターよ！他のみんなはただのおまけ。

idioms.meaning: Participating without having an important role
idioms.meaning_ja: ただ付き合っているだけ、おまけで参加している

dialogues.english: Porsha, the show is a team effort. There's no "I" in team!
dialogues.japanese: ポーシャ、ショーはチームの努力だよ。チームに「自分だけ」はないんだ！

idioms.meaning: Something achieved by working together as a group
idioms.meaning_ja: チームの努力、共同作業

idioms.meaning: Success depends on cooperation, not individual ego
idioms.meaning_ja: チームワークが大事、自分勝手はダメ

dialogues.english: Oh dear, the set pieces are falling apart at the seams!
dialogues.japanese: あらまあ、セットがバラバラになってるわ！

idioms.meaning: Breaking down completely; in very poor condition
idioms.meaning_ja: バラバラになる、崩壊する

dialogues.english: Stay calm, everyone. We'll sort it out. Let's take it from the top.
dialogues.japanese: 落ち着いて、みんな。なんとかするよ。最初からやり直そう。

idioms.meaning: To resolve a problem or organize something
idioms.meaning_ja: 解決する、整理する

idioms.meaning: To start again from the beginning
idioms.meaning_ja: 最初からやり直す

dialogues.english: This is a train wreck! You people are wasting my time!
dialogues.japanese: これは大惨事だ！お前たちは私の時間を無駄にしている！

idioms.meaning: A complete disaster or chaotic situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 大惨事、めちゃくちゃな状態

idioms.meaning: Using time ineffectively or pointlessly
idioms.meaning_ja: 時間を無駄にする

dialogues.english: Don't listen to him, guys. We've come too far to throw it all away.
dialogues.japanese: 彼の言うことは気にするな、みんな。ここまで来て全部捨てるわけにはいかない。

idioms.meaning: To have made too much progress to stop now
idioms.meaning_ja: ここまで来た、もう後戻りできないほど進んだ

idioms.meaning: To waste everything that has been achieved
idioms.meaning_ja: 全部台無しにする、すべてを捨てる

dialogues.english: You've been stringing me along this whole time! Clay Calloway was never coming!
dialogues.japanese: ずっと俺を騙していたのか！クレイ・キャロウェイは最初から来る気がなかったんだ！

idioms.meaning: To deceive someone by making false promises over time
idioms.meaning_ja: 騙し続ける、じらし続ける

dialogues.english: Mr. Crystal, please, give me one more chance. I can still pull this off!
dialogues.japanese: クリスタルさん、お願いです、もう一度チャンスをください。まだやれます！

idioms.meaning: An additional opportunity to succeed
idioms.meaning_ja: もう一度チャンスを

idioms.meaning: To succeed in doing something difficult
idioms.meaning_ja: やり遂げる、成功させる

dialogues.english: You're out, Moon! Pack your bags and hit the road!
dialogues.japanese: お前はクビだ、ムーン！荷物をまとめて出て行け！

idioms.meaning: To prepare to leave
idioms.meaning_ja: 荷物をまとめる、出発の準備をする

idioms.meaning: To leave or go away
idioms.meaning_ja: 出発する、立ち去る

dialogues.english: I know I messed up. I bit off more than I could chew.
dialogues.japanese: 失敗したのは分かってる。身の丈に合わないことをしてしまった。

idioms.meaning: Made a mistake or error
idioms.meaning_ja: 失敗した、しくじった

idioms.expression: bit off more than I could chew
idioms.meaning: Tried to do more than one is capable of
idioms.meaning_ja: 身の丈に合わないことをした

dialogues.english: Don't beat yourself up, Buster. We all had a hand in this.
dialogues.japanese: 自分を責めないで、バスター。私たちみんな関わっていたんだから。

idioms.meaning: To criticize or blame oneself excessively
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分を責める、自己嫌悪に陥る

idioms.meaning: Was involved in or contributed to something
idioms.meaning_ja: 関わっていた、一役買った

dialogues.english: Are we really just going to call it quits? After everything we've been through?
dialogues.japanese: 本当にこれで終わりにするの？今まで乗り越えてきたことの後で？

idioms.meaning: To stop doing something; to give up
idioms.meaning_ja: やめる、終わりにする

idioms.meaning: Experienced, especially something difficult
idioms.meaning_ja: 経験してきた、乗り越えてきた

dialogues.english: No way. We didn't come all this way to give up at the finish line.
dialogues.japanese: ありえない。ゴール手前で諦めるためにここまで来たんじゃない。

idioms.meaning: To have traveled far or made great effort
idioms.meaning_ja: はるばる来た、ここまで頑張ってきた

idioms.meaning: At the very end; just before completing something
idioms.meaning_ja: ゴール目前で、最後の最後で

dialogues.english: We're not going down without a fight!
dialogues.japanese: 戦わずに負けるなんてありえない！

idioms.expression: not going down without a fight
idioms.meaning: Refusing to accept defeat without resisting
idioms.meaning_ja: 戦わずに負けない、最後まで抵抗する

dialogues.english: Buster, we're with you through thick and thin. What's the plan?
dialogues.japanese: バスター、どんな時も一緒よ。計画は何？

idioms.meaning: Through good times and bad times; no matter what
idioms.meaning_ja: どんな時も、良い時も悪い時も

dialogues.english: We put on the show ourselves. No Crystal, no rules. We do it our way.
dialogues.japanese: 自分たちでショーをやるんだ。クリスタルなし、ルールなし。自分たちのやり方で。

idioms.meaning: To proceed according to one's own plan
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分たちのやり方でやる

dialogues.english: Wait... you guys are actually going through with this? Without my dad?
dialogues.japanese: 待って...本当にやるの？パパなしで？

idioms.meaning: To complete something despite difficulties or doubts
idioms.meaning_ja: やり遂げる、最後までやる

dialogues.english: Your dad doesn't call the shots anymore. This is our show now.
dialogues.japanese: もうあなたのパパが仕切る必要はないの。これは私たちのショーよ。

idioms.meaning: To make the important decisions; to be in charge
idioms.meaning_ja: 仕切る、決定権を持つ

dialogues.english: I... I want to be part of it. For real this time. Not because of my dad.
dialogues.japanese: 私...参加したい。今度は本当に。パパのためじゃなく。

idioms.meaning: Genuinely; not pretending
idioms.meaning_ja: 本当に、本気で

dialogues.english: Everyone deserves a second chance, Porsha. Welcome aboard.
dialogues.japanese: 誰にでもセカンドチャンスはある、ポーシャ。ようこそ。

idioms.meaning: Another opportunity after a failure
idioms.meaning_ja: やり直しの機会、もう一度のチャンス

idioms.meaning: Welcome to the team or group
idioms.meaning_ja: ようこそ、チームへようこそ

dialogues.english: I've been riding on my dad's coattails my whole life. It's time to stand on my own two feet.
dialogues.japanese: ずっとパパの七光りに頼ってきた。自分の力で立つ時が来たの。

idioms.meaning: Benefiting from someone else's success or influence
idioms.meaning_ja: 人の七光りに頼る、人のおかげで恩恵を受ける

idioms.expression: stand on my own two feet
idioms.meaning: To be independent and self-sufficient
idioms.meaning_ja: 自立する、自分の力で立つ

dialogues.english: Clay! You came! I didn't think you'd show up!
dialogues.japanese: クレイ！来てくれたの！来てくれると思わなかった！

idioms.meaning: To arrive or appear somewhere
idioms.meaning_ja: 現れる、来る

dialogues.english: Your song got under my skin, kid. I couldn't get it out of my head.
dialogues.japanese: 君の曲が心に刺さったんだ。頭から離れなかった。

idioms.meaning: To affect someone deeply; to bother or move someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 心に刺さる、深く影響を与える

idioms.expression: couldn't get it out of my head
idioms.meaning: Unable to stop thinking about something
idioms.meaning_ja: 頭から離れない

dialogues.english: Ruby always said I was too stubborn for my own good. Maybe it's time I turned over a new leaf.
dialogues.japanese: ルビーはいつも言ってた、頑固すぎて損してるって。そろそろ心機一転する時かもしれない。

idioms.expression: too stubborn for my own good
idioms.meaning: So stubborn that it causes problems for oneself
idioms.meaning_ja: 頑固すぎて自分で損をする

idioms.meaning: To start behaving in a better way; to make a fresh start
idioms.meaning_ja: 心機一転する、新しいスタートを切る

dialogues.english: Mr. Calloway! I can't believe my eyes! This is a dream come true!
dialogues.japanese: キャロウェイさん！目を疑うよ！夢が叶った！

idioms.meaning: To be extremely surprised by what one sees
idioms.meaning_ja: 目を疑う、信じられない

idioms.meaning: Something wonderful that one has wished for
idioms.meaning_ja: 夢が叶う、念願が実現する

dialogues.english: Don't get too carried away, kid. I'm a bit rusty. Haven't played in ages.
dialogues.japanese: あまり興奮するな。少し腕がなまってる。長い間演奏してないからな。

idioms.meaning: To become too excited or enthusiastic
idioms.meaning_ja: 興奮しすぎる、我を忘れる

idioms.meaning: Having lost skill due to lack of practice
idioms.meaning_ja: 腕がなまっている、ブランクがある

idioms.meaning: For a very long time
idioms.meaning_ja: 長い間、久しぶりに

dialogues.english: You'll pick it up again in no time. It's like riding a bike.
dialogues.japanese: すぐに取り戻せるよ。自転車と同じで、一度覚えたら忘れないから。

idioms.meaning: To resume or recover a skill
idioms.meaning_ja: 取り戻す、再開する

idioms.meaning: Very quickly
idioms.meaning_ja: すぐに、あっという間に

idioms.meaning: Something you never forget how to do once learned
idioms.meaning_ja: 一度覚えたら忘れない

dialogues.english: I'm doing this for Ruby. She wouldn't have wanted me to throw in the towel.
dialogues.japanese: ルビーのためにやるんだ。彼女は俺に諦めてほしくなかったはずだ。

dialogues.english: We're pulling out all the stops for this one. This is do or die.
dialogues.japanese: 全力を尽くすぞ。やるかやられるかだ。

idioms.meaning: To make every possible effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 全力を尽くす、あらゆる手を使う

idioms.meaning: A situation where one must succeed or face serious consequences
idioms.meaning_ja: やるかやられるか、一か八か

dialogues.english: I pulled some strings and got us into the Crystal Theater tonight.
dialogues.japanese: コネを使って今夜クリスタル・シアターを押さえたわ。

idioms.meaning: To use one's influence or connections to get something done
idioms.meaning_ja: コネを使う、裏から手を回す

dialogues.english: Suki, you're a lifesaver! I owe you one.
dialogues.japanese: スキ、命の恩人だ！借りができたね。

idioms.meaning: A person who helps in a critical moment
idioms.meaning_ja: 命の恩人、大いに助かる人

idioms.meaning: I am indebted to you for a favor
idioms.meaning_ja: 借りができた、恩に着るよ

dialogues.english: The sets aren't perfect, but they'll do the trick!
dialogues.japanese: セットは完璧じゃないけど、十分いけるよ！

idioms.meaning: To achieve the desired result
idioms.meaning_ja: うまくいく、効果がある

dialogues.english: Johnny and I have been burning the midnight oil on the choreography.
dialogues.japanese: ジョニーと私は深夜まで振り付けを練習してたの。

idioms.meaning: Working late into the night
idioms.meaning_ja: 深夜まで働く、夜遅くまで頑張る

dialogues.english: I finally nailed the routine! It took blood, sweat, and tears, but I got there.
dialogues.japanese: ついにルーティンを完璧にしたよ！血と汗と涙が必要だったけど、やり遂げた。

idioms.meaning: To do something perfectly
idioms.meaning_ja: 完璧にやった、見事に成功した

idioms.meaning: Extremely hard work and effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 血と汗と涙、非常な努力

dialogues.english: I'm still nervous, but I'm not going to let fear hold me back anymore.
dialogues.japanese: まだ緊張してるけど、もう恐怖に足を引っ張られないわ。

idioms.meaning: To prevent someone from making progress
idioms.meaning_ja: 足を引っ張る、妨げる

dialogues.english: I've faced my fears head-on. I'm ready to give it everything I've got.
dialogues.japanese: 恐怖に正面から向き合ったわ。持てるすべてを出す準備ができてる。

idioms.meaning: Directly and without avoiding
idioms.meaning_ja: 正面から、真っ向から

idioms.meaning: All of one's ability and effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 持てるすべて、全力

dialogues.english: This is the moment of truth. Everything we've worked for comes down to this.
dialogues.japanese: これが真実の瞬間だ。僕たちが頑張ってきたすべてがこの一瞬にかかっている。

idioms.meaning: A critical time when an important decision or outcome is revealed
idioms.meaning_ja: 真実の瞬間、正念場

idioms.meaning: Everything depends on this one thing
idioms.meaning_ja: これにかかっている、これ次第だ

dialogues.english: Let's blow the roof off this place!
dialogues.japanese: この場所の屋根を吹き飛ばそう！

idioms.meaning: To create an extremely exciting atmosphere
idioms.meaning_ja: 会場を大いに盛り上げる、屋根が吹き飛ぶほど盛り上げる

dialogues.english: No matter what happens out there, I just want you all to know... it's been a blast.
dialogues.japanese: 何が起ころうと、みんなに言いたいの...最高に楽しかった。

idioms.meaning: Regardless of what happens
idioms.meaning_ja: 何が起ころうと、どんなことがあっても

idioms.meaning: It has been a very enjoyable experience
idioms.meaning_ja: 最高に楽しかった、すごく良い経験だった

dialogues.english: Same here. You guys are like family to me. Let's leave it all on the stage.
dialogues.japanese: 僕もだよ。みんなは家族みたいだ。ステージにすべてを出し切ろう。

idioms.expression: leave it all on the stage
idioms.meaning: To give absolutely everything in a performance
idioms.meaning_ja: ステージにすべてを出し切る、全力を尽くす

dialogues.english: I'm not going to hold back tonight. This is my time to shine.
dialogues.japanese: 今夜は遠慮しない。これが私の輝く時。

idioms.meaning: To restrain oneself; to not use full effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 遠慮する、抑える

idioms.meaning: An opportunity to show one's abilities
idioms.meaning_ja: 輝く時、実力を見せる時

dialogues.english: I've got a new lease on life after this journey. Win or lose, it was worth it.
dialogues.japanese: この旅を経て新しい人生を手に入れた気分。勝っても負けても、価値があったわ。

idioms.meaning: A chance to enjoy life more, especially after a difficult time
idioms.meaning_ja: 新しい人生、生きがいを取り戻す

idioms.meaning: Regardless of the outcome
idioms.meaning_ja: 勝っても負けても

dialogues.english: I haven't felt this alive in years. Let's do this.
dialogues.japanese: こんなに生きていると感じたのは何年ぶりだ。やるぞ。

idioms.meaning: Experienced such a strong sense of vitality and excitement
idioms.meaning_ja: こんなに生きていると感じた、活力に満ちた

dialogues.english: Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to our show! Sit back and enjoy the ride!
dialogues.japanese: 皆様、ショーへようこそ！ゆっくり座って楽しんでください！

idioms.expression: sit back and enjoy the ride
idioms.meaning: To relax and enjoy an experience
idioms.meaning_ja: ゆっくり楽しむ、リラックスして楽しむ

dialogues.english: I never thought I'd be up here on my own merits. This feels incredible.
dialogues.japanese: 自分の実力でここに立てるとは思わなかった。信じられない気分。

idioms.meaning: Based on one's own abilities, not because of connections
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分の実力で、自力で

dialogues.english: The crowd is going wild! We're killing it out there!
dialogues.japanese: 観客が大盛り上がりだ！僕たち最高にイケてる！

idioms.meaning: Becoming extremely excited or enthusiastic
idioms.meaning_ja: 大盛り上がりだ、熱狂している

idioms.meaning: Performing extremely well
idioms.meaning_ja: 最高にうまくやっている、大成功している

dialogues.english: I can't believe I'm singing my heart out in front of all these people!
dialogues.japanese: こんなに大勢の前で全力で歌ってるなんて信じられない！

idioms.meaning: Singing with great emotion and energy
idioms.meaning_ja: 全力で歌う、心を込めて歌う

dialogues.english: The special effects are working like a charm! Everything is falling into place!
dialogues.japanese: 特殊効果がバッチリ動いてる！すべてがうまくいってる！

idioms.meaning: Perfectly; very effectively
idioms.meaning_ja: バッチリ、完璧に

idioms.meaning: Happening in a satisfactory way
idioms.meaning_ja: うまくいく、ぴったり合う

dialogues.english: What is this?! You went behind my back! This is MY theater!
dialogues.japanese: なんだこれは！？俺の知らないところでやりやがって！ここは俺のシアターだ！

idioms.meaning: Did something secretly without telling someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 陰でこそこそやった、内緒でやった

dialogues.english: The show must go on, Mr. Crystal. And nothing you do can stop it.
dialogues.japanese: ショーは続けなければならない、クリスタルさん。あなたが何をしても止められない。

idioms.meaning: An event or activity must continue despite problems
idioms.meaning_ja: ショーは続けなければならない、何があっても続ける

dialogues.english: You'll pay for this, Moon! You're playing with fire!
dialogues.japanese: この借りは返すぞ、ムーン！火遊びをしているんだぞ！

idioms.meaning: You will suffer consequences for your actions
idioms.meaning_ja: この借りは返す、覚えていろ

idioms.meaning: Taking a dangerous risk
idioms.meaning_ja: 火遊びをする、危険なことをする

dialogues.english: Dad, stop it! For once in your life, let someone else have the spotlight!
dialogues.japanese: パパ、やめて！一度くらい他の人にスポットライトを譲ってよ！

idioms.meaning: Used to emphasize that someone should do something they never do
idioms.meaning_ja: 一度くらい、たまには

idioms.meaning: To receive public attention
idioms.meaning_ja: スポットライトを浴びる、注目を受ける

dialogues.english: This one's for you, Ruby. I should have done this a long time ago.
dialogues.japanese: これは君のために、ルビー。もっと早くやるべきだった。

idioms.meaning: Dedicating a performance or action to someone
idioms.meaning_ja: これは君のために

idioms.meaning: In the distant past
idioms.meaning_ja: ずっと前に

dialogues.english: He's pouring his heart into every note. There isn't a dry eye in the house.
dialogues.japanese: 一音一音に心を込めている。会場中が涙を流してる。

idioms.meaning: Putting all of one's emotion and effort into something
idioms.meaning_ja: 心を込める、感情を注ぐ

idioms.expression: not a dry eye in the house
idioms.meaning: Everyone in the audience is crying (from emotion)
idioms.meaning_ja: 会場中が涙、観客全員が泣いている

dialogues.english: I've got goosebumps. This is giving me chills.
dialogues.japanese: 鳥肌が立つ。ゾクゾクする。

idioms.meaning: Tiny bumps on the skin caused by strong emotion
idioms.meaning_ja: 鳥肌

idioms.meaning: Causing a strong emotional reaction
idioms.meaning_ja: ゾクゾクする、感動で震える

dialogues.english: This is everything I've ever dreamed of. It was all worth it in the end.
dialogues.japanese: これが僕がずっと夢見ていたことだ。最後には全部報われた。

idioms.meaning: The result justified all the effort and difficulty
idioms.meaning_ja: 最後には報われた、結果的にやった甲斐があった

dialogues.english: We did it! The audience is on their feet! Standing ovation!
dialogues.japanese: やったぞ！観客がスタンディングオベーションしてる！

idioms.meaning: Standing up, especially to applaud
idioms.meaning_ja: 立ち上がって、スタンディングオベーション

dialogues.english: We pulled it off! Against all odds!
dialogues.japanese: やり遂げた！あらゆる困難に打ち勝って！

idioms.meaning: Succeeded in doing something difficult
idioms.meaning_ja: やり遂げた、成功させた

idioms.meaning: Despite very low chances of success
idioms.meaning_ja: あらゆる困難に打ち勝って、万難を排して

dialogues.english: I can't believe we're getting a standing ovation! This is the cherry on top!
dialogues.japanese: スタンディングオベーションをもらってるなんて信じられない！最高の締めくくりだ！

idioms.meaning: Something that makes a good situation even better
idioms.meaning_ja: 最高の仕上げ、ケーキの上のさくらんぼ

dialogues.english: I faced my biggest fear and came out on top. I'm on cloud nine!
dialogues.japanese: 最大の恐怖に立ち向かって勝ったわ。最高に幸せ！

idioms.meaning: To be the winner or most successful
idioms.meaning_ja: 勝利を収めた、一番になった

idioms.meaning: Extremely happy
idioms.meaning_ja: 最高に幸せ、有頂天

dialogues.english: I proved to myself that I still have it in me. Never too late to follow your dreams.
dialogues.japanese: まだやれるって自分に証明できた。夢を追うのに遅すぎることはないのね。

idioms.meaning: To have the ability or potential to do something
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分にその力がある、やれる

idioms.meaning: It is always possible to start something or make a change
idioms.meaning_ja: 遅すぎることはない

dialogues.english: Thank you, kid. You gave me a reason to get back in the game.
dialogues.japanese: ありがとう、お嬢ちゃん。また戦場に戻る理由をくれた。

idioms.meaning: To return to active participation after a period away
idioms.meaning_ja: 復帰する、また戦場に戻る

dialogues.english: We are the talk of the town! Everyone is buzzing about our show!
dialogues.japanese: 僕たちは街の話題だ！みんながショーの噂で持ちきりだ！

idioms.meaning: The subject of much conversation
idioms.meaning_ja: 街の話題、みんなの噂の的

idioms.meaning: Talking excitedly about something
idioms.meaning_ja: 噂で持ちきり、話題にしている

dialogues.english: For the first time, I feel like I've found my own voice. Not daddy's little girl anymore.
dialogues.japanese: 初めて、自分自身の声を見つけた気がする。もうパパのお嬢ちゃんじゃない。

idioms.meaning: Discovered one's unique identity or way of expression
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分自身の声を見つけた、自分らしさを発見した

dialogues.english: Oh, Mr. Moon! I'm so proud! You always have an ace up your sleeve!
dialogues.japanese: ああ、ムーンさん！とても誇りに思います！いつも切り札を隠し持ってるのね！

idioms.meaning: A secret advantage or resource kept in reserve
idioms.meaning_ja: 切り札、隠し持った秘策

dialogues.english: I couldn't have done it without each and every one of you. You're the real stars.
dialogues.japanese: みんな一人一人がいなければできなかった。君たちこそが本当のスターだ。

idioms.meaning: Would not have been possible without someone's help
idioms.meaning_ja: 〜なしでは成し遂げられなかった

dialogues.english: Who would have thought that a little theater troupe from nowhere would end up here?
dialogues.japanese: 名もない小さな劇団がここに辿り着くなんて、誰が想像しただろう？

idioms.meaning: An expression of surprise at an unexpected outcome
idioms.meaning_ja: 誰が想像しただろう、まさか

idioms.meaning: From an unknown or unimportant place
idioms.meaning_ja: 無名の、どこからともなく

idioms.meaning: To finally be in a particular situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 結局〜になる、行き着く

dialogues.english: We've come a long way from the Moon Theater. But the best is yet to come.
dialogues.japanese: ムーン・シアターから長い道のりを来たね。でも一番いいことはこれからだ。

idioms.meaning: To have made great progress
idioms.meaning_ja: 大きく成長した、長い道のりを歩んできた

idioms.expression: the best is yet to come
idioms.meaning: The most wonderful things have not happened yet
idioms.meaning_ja: 一番いいことはこれから、最高の時はまだ来ていない

dialogues.english: I've learned that it's okay to ask for help. You don't have to go it alone.
dialogues.japanese: 助けを求めていいんだって学んだよ。一人でやる必要はない。

idioms.meaning: To do something by oneself without help
idioms.meaning_ja: 一人でやる、独力でやる

dialogues.english: I found my voice, literally and figuratively. I'm no longer afraid to speak up.
dialogues.japanese: 文字通り、そして比喩的にも自分の声を見つけた。もう声を上げることが怖くない。

idioms.meaning: In both the exact and symbolic sense
idioms.meaning_ja: 文字通り、そして比喩的にも

idioms.meaning: To express one's opinion openly
idioms.meaning_ja: 声を上げる、はっきり意見を言う

dialogues.english: Being a mom and a performer isn't easy, but I wouldn't trade it for the world.
dialogues.japanese: 母親とパフォーマーの両立は簡単じゃないけど、何にも代えがたいわ。

idioms.expression: wouldn't trade it for the world
idioms.meaning: Would not give it up for anything
idioms.meaning_ja: 何にも代えがたい、絶対に手放したくない

dialogues.english: Music isn't just about the past. It's about looking ahead. Ruby taught me that.
dialogues.japanese: 音楽は過去だけのものじゃない。前を向くことだ。ルビーがそれを教えてくれた。

idioms.meaning: Thinking about and planning for the future
idioms.meaning_ja: 前を向く、未来に目を向ける

dialogues.english: Next stop: world tour! The sky's the limit!
dialogues.japanese: 次はワールドツアーだ！可能性は無限大！

idioms.meaning: There is no limit to what can be achieved
idioms.meaning_ja: 可能性は無限大、限界はない

dialogues.english: Whatever comes next, we'll face it together. After all, the show must go on!
dialogues.japanese: 次に何が来ようと、一緒に立ち向かおう。結局、ショーは続けなければならないんだ！

idioms.meaning: No matter what happens in the future
idioms.meaning_ja: 次に何が来ようと

idioms.meaning: Used to add a final reason or to remind of an important fact
idioms.meaning_ja: 結局、何と言っても

dialogues.english: In this town, it's sink or swim. And you're barely treading water.
dialogues.japanese: この街では生きるか死ぬかだ。お前はかろうじて浮いているだけだ。

idioms.meaning: To either fail or succeed by one's own efforts
idioms.meaning_ja: 生きるか死ぬか、自力で何とかするしかない

idioms.meaning: Making no progress; just barely surviving
idioms.meaning_ja: かろうじて生き延びている、進歩していない

dialogues.english: You've got guts, Moon. Not many people have the nerve to stand up to Crystal.
dialogues.japanese: 度胸があるわね、ムーン。クリスタルに立ち向かう勇気のある人はそうそういないわ。

idioms.meaning: To have courage
idioms.meaning_ja: 度胸がある、勇気がある

idioms.meaning: To have the courage or audacity to do something
idioms.meaning_ja: 〜する勇気がある、〜する度胸がある

idioms.meaning: To resist or confront someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 立ち向かう、対抗する

dialogues.english: Life is too short to play by someone else's rules. Make your own path.
dialogues.japanese: 他人のルールに従うには人生は短すぎる。自分の道を作りなよ。

idioms.meaning: Used to say something is not worth worrying about
idioms.meaning_ja: 人生は短い、くよくよしてる暇はない

idioms.meaning: To follow established guidelines
idioms.meaning_ja: ルールに従う

idioms.meaning: To create your own way in life
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分の道を切り開く

dialogues.english: Buster has always been a dreamer. But that's what makes him tick.
dialogues.japanese: バスターはいつも夢見がちだ。でもそれが彼の原動力なんだ。

idioms.meaning: What motivates or drives someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 彼の原動力、何が彼を動かしているか

dialogues.english: Meena, you knocked it out of the park tonight. You were absolutely brilliant.
dialogues.japanese: ミーナ、今夜は大成功だったよ。本当に素晴らしかった。

idioms.expression: knocked it out of the park
idioms.meaning: To achieve something exceptionally well (from baseball)
idioms.meaning_ja: 大成功を収めた、ホームランを打った

dialogues.english: When one door closes, another one opens. That's what show business is all about.
dialogues.japanese: 一つのドアが閉まれば、別のドアが開く。それがショービジネスってものさ。

idioms.expression: when one door closes, another one opens
idioms.meaning: New opportunities arise when others end
idioms.meaning_ja: 一つのドアが閉まれば別のドアが開く、捨てる神あれば拾う神あり

dialogues.english: You can't run from your past forever. At some point, you gotta face the music.
dialogues.japanese: 過去から永遠に逃げることはできない。いつかは報いを受け入れなきゃならない。

idioms.meaning: To try to avoid dealing with past experiences
idioms.meaning_ja: 過去から逃げる

idioms.meaning: To accept the consequences of one's actions
idioms.meaning_ja: 報いを受け入れる、結果に向き合う

dialogues.english: I may be a mom of twenty-five, but I've still got a few tricks up my sleeve.
dialogues.japanese: 25人の子供の母親かもしれないけど、まだ秘策はあるのよ。

idioms.meaning: Secret skills or plans held in reserve
idioms.meaning_ja: 秘策がある、隠し技がある

dialogues.english: Dream big, dance bigger! That's my motto!
dialogues.japanese: 大きく夢を見て、もっと大きく踊れ！それが僕のモットーだ！

idioms.meaning: To have ambitious goals and aspirations
idioms.meaning_ja: 大きな夢を持つ

dialogues.english: Music has the power to heal. It's what keeps me going when times get tough.
dialogues.japanese: 音楽には癒す力がある。辛い時に前に進ませてくれるの。

idioms.meaning: What motivates or sustains someone during difficult times
idioms.meaning_ja: 前に進ませてくれる、支えになっている

idioms.meaning: When situations become difficult
idioms.meaning_ja: 辛い時、困難な時期

dialogues.english: I built this empire from the ground up. Nobody pulls the wool over my eyes.
dialogues.japanese: この帝国をゼロから築いた。誰にも騙されはしない。

idioms.meaning: From the very beginning; starting from nothing
idioms.meaning_ja: ゼロから、一から

idioms.expression: pull the wool over my eyes
idioms.meaning: To deceive or trick someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 騙す、目をくらます

dialogues.english: Every setback is a setup for a comeback. That's what I truly believe.
dialogues.japanese: すべての挫折は復活への準備だ。本当にそう信じているよ。

idioms.expression: setback is a setup for a comeback
idioms.meaning: Failures prepare you for future success
idioms.meaning_ja: 挫折は復活への準備、失敗は成功のもと

dialogues.english: I didn't get where I am by being soft. In this business, you eat or get eaten.
dialogues.japanese: 甘いことをしてここまで来たわけじゃない。この業界では、食うか食われるかだ。

idioms.meaning: A highly competitive environment where only the strong survive
idioms.meaning_ja: 食うか食われるか、弱肉強食

dialogues.english: My reputation is on the line. This show better deliver, or heads will roll.
dialogues.japanese: 俺の評判がかかっている。このショーは成功しなきゃ、ただじゃおかないぞ。

idioms.meaning: At risk of being lost or damaged
idioms.meaning_ja: かかっている、危険にさらされている

idioms.meaning: People will be punished or fired
idioms.meaning_ja: 誰かの首が飛ぶ、責任を取らされる

dialogues.english: Crystal doesn't take kindly to broken promises. He's got a mean streak a mile wide.
dialogues.japanese: クリスタルは約束を破ることを許さないわ。彼は底意地が悪いのよ。

idioms.meaning: Reacts negatively to something
idioms.meaning_ja: 〜を快く思わない、〜を許さない

idioms.meaning: A tendency to be cruel or unkind
idioms.meaning_ja: 意地悪な面、底意地の悪さ

dialogues.english: The streets are where the real magic happens. No fancy stage, just raw talent.
dialogues.japanese: ストリートこそ本当の魔法が起きる場所。豪華なステージなんていらない、生の才能だけ。

idioms.meaning: Natural ability that has not been formally developed
idioms.meaning_ja: 生の才能、天性の才能

dialogues.english: Don't overthink it, Johnny. Sometimes you just gotta wing it.
dialogues.japanese: 考えすぎないで、ジョニー。時にはぶっつけ本番でやるしかないの。

idioms.meaning: To think about something too much or too carefully
idioms.meaning_ja: 考えすぎる

idioms.meaning: To improvise without preparation
idioms.meaning_ja: ぶっつけ本番でやる、即興でやる

dialogues.english: I can't believe how much I've improved! You really brought out the best in me.
dialogues.japanese: こんなに上達するなんて信じられない！君が僕の最高を引き出してくれたんだ。

idioms.expression: brought out the best in me
idioms.meaning: Helped someone achieve their full potential
idioms.meaning_ja: 最高の自分を引き出してくれた

dialogues.english: It was always there. You just needed someone to show you the ropes.
dialogues.japanese: ずっとあったんだよ。誰かにコツを教えてもらう必要があっただけ。

idioms.meaning: To teach someone the basic ways of doing a task
idioms.meaning_ja: コツを教える、やり方を教える

dialogues.english: Buster, you're spreading yourself too thin. You can't do everything by yourself.
dialogues.japanese: バスター、手を広げすぎだ。一人で全部はできないよ。

idioms.meaning: Trying to do too many things at once, reducing quality
idioms.meaning_ja: 手を広げすぎる、あれもこれもやりすぎる

dialogues.english: I know, but I'm the one who got us into this mess. The buck stops with me.
dialogues.japanese: 分かってるけど、僕がこの問題に巻き込んだんだ。最終的な責任は僕にある。

idioms.meaning: Caused the current problematic situation
idioms.meaning_ja: この面倒に巻き込んだ

idioms.meaning: I take full responsibility
idioms.meaning_ja: 最終的な責任は自分にある

dialogues.english: That's what friends are for, buddy. We sink or swim together.
dialogues.japanese: そのための友達だろ。一蓮托生だよ。

idioms.meaning: Friends are meant to help each other in difficult times
idioms.meaning_ja: そのための友達だ

dialogues.english: I may be old, but I've still got a spring in my step!
dialogues.japanese: 年は取ったけど、まだまだ元気よ！

idioms.meaning: A lively, energetic way of walking; enthusiasm
idioms.meaning_ja: 足取りが軽い、元気がある

dialogues.english: Miss Crawly, you're one in a million. What would I do without you?
dialogues.japanese: ミス・クローリー、あなたは百万人に一人だ。あなたなしでどうするんだ？

idioms.meaning: Very special or unique
idioms.meaning_ja: 百万人に一人、非常に特別な

dialogues.english: Clay, I know you're hurting. But bottling up your feelings won't make the pain go away.
dialogues.japanese: クレイ、辛いのは分かる。でも感情を押し殺しても痛みは消えない。

idioms.meaning: Suppressing or hiding one's emotions
idioms.meaning_ja: 感情を押し殺す、気持ちを抑え込む

idioms.meaning: To eliminate suffering
idioms.meaning_ja: 痛みを消す、苦しみをなくす

dialogues.english: When Ruby died, a part of me died too. I've been going through the motions ever since.
dialogues.japanese: ルビーが死んだ時、俺の一部も死んだ。それ以来、ただ惰性で生きてきた。

idioms.meaning: Doing something mechanically without real interest or effort
idioms.meaning_ja: 惰性でやる、形だけやる

dialogues.english: Sometimes you have to hit rock bottom before you can rise again.
dialogues.japanese: また立ち上がるためには、一度どん底に落ちなきゃいけない時もある。

idioms.meaning: To reach the lowest possible point
idioms.meaning_ja: どん底に落ちる、最低の状態になる

dialogues.english: You remind me of Ruby, you know that? She never took no for an answer either.
dialogues.japanese: ルビーを思い出すよ。彼女もノーとは言わせない人だった。

idioms.expression: never took no for an answer
idioms.meaning: Refusing to accept rejection; very persistent
idioms.meaning_ja: ノーとは言わせない、諦めない

dialogues.english: Alfonso, you make me feel at ease. Like I can be myself around you.
dialogues.japanese: アルフォンソ、あなたといると安心する。ありのままの自分でいられる。

idioms.meaning: To feel relaxed and comfortable
idioms.meaning_ja: 安心する、くつろぐ

idioms.meaning: To behave naturally without pretending
idioms.meaning_ja: ありのままの自分でいる

dialogues.english: That's the real you, Meena. Don't ever let anyone dull your sparkle.
dialogues.japanese: それが本当のミーナだよ。誰にも輝きを曇らせないで。

idioms.meaning: To diminish someone's enthusiasm or unique qualities
idioms.meaning_ja: 輝きを失わせる、個性を曇らせる

dialogues.english: You think you can cut corners and still make it? This is the big time!
dialogues.japanese: 手を抜いても成功できると思っているのか？ここは一流の世界だぞ！

idioms.meaning: To do something in a cheaper or easier way, often reducing quality
idioms.meaning_ja: 手を抜く、近道をする

idioms.meaning: The highest level of success or fame
idioms.meaning_ja: 一流の世界、大舞台

dialogues.english: I'm not cutting corners. I'm doing things my way, and it's paying off.
dialogues.japanese: 手を抜いてない。自分のやり方でやっていて、それが成果を出している。

idioms.meaning: According to one's own method or preference
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分のやり方で

idioms.meaning: Producing good results
idioms.meaning_ja: 成果が出ている、報われている

dialogues.english: Mark my words, this amateur act will fall flat on its face.
dialogues.japanese: 俺の言葉を覚えておけ、この素人芝居は惨めに失敗するぞ。

idioms.meaning: Remember what I'm saying (because it will come true)
idioms.meaning_ja: 俺の言葉を覚えておけ、言ったとおりになるぞ

idioms.meaning: To fail completely and embarrassingly
idioms.meaning_ja: 惨めに失敗する、大コケする

dialogues.english: Don't hold your breath, Klaus. Johnny's got more talent in his little finger than you have in your whole body.
dialogues.japanese: 期待しないことね、クラウス。ジョニーの小指の方があんたの全身より才能があるわ。

idioms.meaning: Don't expect it to happen (sarcastic)
idioms.meaning_ja: 期待しないことね、あてにしない方がいい

idioms.expression: more talent in his little finger
idioms.meaning: Far more talented than someone else (exaggeration)
idioms.meaning_ja: 小指一本の方が才能がある（誇張表現）

dialogues.english: I can't sleep. I've got a million things running through my mind.
dialogues.japanese: 眠れない。頭の中で百万のことが駆け巡っている。

idioms.meaning: Thoughts that keep occurring repeatedly
idioms.meaning_ja: 頭の中を駆け巡る、次々と考えが浮かぶ

dialogues.english: Get some shut-eye, Buster. You're going to need it. Tomorrow is the big day.
dialogues.japanese: 少し寝ろよ、バスター。必要になるぞ。明日は大事な日だ。

idioms.meaning: To get some sleep
idioms.meaning_ja: 少し寝る、仮眠を取る

idioms.meaning: An important or special day
idioms.meaning_ja: 大事な日、決戦の日

dialogues.english: What if it all goes south? What if we crash and burn?
dialogues.japanese: もし全部うまくいかなかったら？大失敗したらどうする？

idioms.meaning: To go wrong; to deteriorate
idioms.meaning_ja: うまくいかなくなる、悪化する

idioms.meaning: To fail spectacularly
idioms.meaning_ja: 大失敗する、派手に失敗する

dialogues.english: Then we pick up the pieces and try again. That's what we always do.
dialogues.japanese: そしたら立て直して、またやるんだ。いつもそうしてきただろ。

idioms.meaning: To recover from a difficult or failed situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 立て直す、やり直す

dialogues.english: I wrote one last song for Clay. It's a long shot, but I think it might seal the deal.
dialogues.japanese: クレイのために最後の一曲を書いた。望みは薄いけど、これで決まるかもしれない。

idioms.meaning: Something unlikely to succeed but worth trying
idioms.meaning_ja: 望みの薄い試み、可能性は低い

idioms.meaning: To finalize an agreement or ensure success
idioms.meaning_ja: 取引を成立させる、決定的にする

dialogues.english: I called my kids tonight. Hearing their voices gave me a second wind.
dialogues.japanese: 今夜子供たちに電話したの。声を聞いたら、また元気が湧いてきたわ。

idioms.meaning: A renewed burst of energy after feeling tired
idioms.meaning_ja: 新たな活力、息を吹き返す

dialogues.english: Win or lose, I've grown so much. I'm not the same person who started this journey.
dialogues.japanese: 勝っても負けても、すごく成長した。この旅を始めた時の自分とは違う。

dialogues.english: Tomorrow, I'm going to put my best foot forward. No more hiding.
dialogues.japanese: 明日は、最善を尽くす。もう隠れない。

idioms.meaning: To make the best possible impression
idioms.meaning_ja: 最善を尽くす、全力で臨む

dialogues.english: Look, I know I've been running around like a chicken with its head cut off.
dialogues.japanese: 聞いて、僕がパニック状態で走り回ってたのは分かってる。

idioms.expression: like a chicken with its head cut off
idioms.meaning: In a frantic, disorganized manner
idioms.meaning_ja: パニック状態で、大慌てで

dialogues.english: But looking at all of you... I know in my gut that we're going to nail this.
dialogues.japanese: でもみんなを見ていると...直感で分かる、きっとうまくいくって。

idioms.meaning: To have a strong intuitive feeling
idioms.meaning_ja: 直感で分かる、腹の底から感じる

idioms.meaning: To accomplish something perfectly
idioms.meaning_ja: 完璧にやる、成功させる

dialogues.english: We've got each other. That's all we need. Let's go out with a bang!
dialogues.japanese: お互いがいる。それだけで十分。派手にいこう！

idioms.meaning: To end in a spectacular, impressive way
idioms.meaning_ja: 派手に締めくくる、華々しく終わる

dialogues.english: This isn't over, Moon! I'll have the last laugh!
dialogues.japanese: これは終わりじゃないぞ、ムーン！最後に笑うのは俺だ！

idioms.meaning: To ultimately succeed after others expected failure
idioms.meaning_ja: 最後に笑う、最終的に勝利する

dialogues.english: Daddy, it's over. You need to face facts. The audience loved THEIR show.
dialogues.japanese: パパ、終わったの。現実を受け入れて。観客は彼らのショーを愛したのよ。

idioms.meaning: To accept the truth of a situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 現実を受け入れる、事実に向き合う

dialogues.english: You're turning your back on your own father?
dialogues.japanese: 自分の父親に背を向けるのか？

idioms.meaning: To abandon or reject someone
idioms.meaning_ja: 背を向ける、見捨てる

dialogues.english: No, I'm standing up for what's right. For the first time, I'm my own person.
dialogues.japanese: いいえ、正しいことのために立ち上がってるの。初めて、自分自身でいられる。

idioms.meaning: Defending or supporting a principle or person
idioms.meaning_ja: 〜のために立ち上がる、〜を支持する

idioms.meaning: Independent; making one's own decisions
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分自身でいる、自立した人間

dialogues.english: From the bottom of my heart, thank you all. We came, we saw, we conquered!
dialogues.japanese: 心の底から、みんなありがとう。来て、見て、征服した！

idioms.expression: from the bottom of my heart
idioms.meaning: With complete sincerity
idioms.meaning_ja: 心の底から、心から

idioms.expression: we came, we saw, we conquered
idioms.meaning: We arrived and achieved victory (quote from Julius Caesar)
idioms.meaning_ja: 来た、見た、勝った（ジュリアス・シーザーの名言）

dialogues.english: This calls for a celebration! Let's paint the town red!
dialogues.japanese: お祝いだ！街を大いに盛り上がろう！

idioms.meaning: Deserves to be celebrated
idioms.meaning_ja: お祝いに値する

idioms.meaning: To go out and celebrate wildly
idioms.meaning_ja: 街で大いに騒ぐ、派手に遊ぶ

dialogues.english: I'd forgotten what it feels like to be part of something bigger than yourself. You brought me back to life.
dialogues.japanese: 自分より大きなものの一部であることがどんな気持ちか忘れていた。君たちが俺を生き返らせてくれた。

idioms.meaning: Something more important than individual concerns
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分より大きなもの

idioms.meaning: Restored someone's vitality or interest in living
idioms.meaning_ja: 生き返らせた、活力を取り戻させた

dialogues.english: That's a wrap, everybody! What a ride it's been!
dialogues.japanese: みんな、これで終わりだ！なんて旅だったんだろう！

idioms.meaning: That's the end of the work (originally from filmmaking)
idioms.meaning_ja: これで終了、お疲れ様

idioms.meaning: What an exciting or eventful experience
idioms.meaning_ja: なんて体験だ、すごい経験だった

dialogues.english: I know it's a tall order, but I have faith in every single one of you.
dialogues.japanese: 無茶な注文だとは分かってるけど、一人一人を信じてるよ。

idioms.meaning: A very demanding or difficult request
idioms.meaning_ja: 無茶な注文、難しい要求

idioms.meaning: To trust and believe in someone or something
idioms.meaning_ja: 信じている、信頼している

dialogues.english: Don't try to butter me up. I see right through you.
dialogues.japanese: ゴマをすろうとするな。お前の考えはお見通しだ。

idioms.meaning: To flatter someone to gain their favor
idioms.meaning_ja: ゴマをする、お世辞を言う

idioms.meaning: To understand someone's true intentions despite deception
idioms.meaning_ja: お見通しだ、本心を見抜いている

dialogues.english: I had a heart-to-heart with Clay. I think he's coming around.
dialogues.japanese: クレイと腹を割って話した。彼も気持ちが変わってきてると思う。

idioms.meaning: A sincere, intimate conversation
idioms.meaning_ja: 腹を割った話、胸襟を開いた会話

idioms.meaning: Gradually changing one's opinion or attitude
idioms.meaning_ja: 考えが変わってきている、だんだん受け入れている

dialogues.english: I miss my family, but I have to see this through to the end.
dialogues.japanese: 家族が恋しいけど、最後までやり遂げなきゃ。

idioms.meaning: To continue with something until it is finished
idioms.meaning_ja: 最後までやり遂げる

dialogues.english: I used to be scared of my own shadow. Now look at me up on that stage.
dialogues.japanese: 昔は自分の影にも怯えていた。今、ステージに立っている私を見て。

idioms.meaning: Extremely timid or fearful
idioms.meaning_ja: 自分の影にも怯える、極度に臆病な

dialogues.english: When life gives you lemons, make lemonade! And then dance!
dialogues.japanese: 人生がレモンをくれたら、レモネードを作れ！そして踊れ！

idioms.expression: when life gives you lemons, make lemonade
idioms.meaning: Make the best of a bad situation
idioms.meaning_ja: 逆境を逆手に取る、悪い状況を良いものに変える

dialogues.english: The press is having a field day with this story. You're front-page news.
dialogues.japanese: マスコミがこの話で大喜びしてるわ。あなたたちは一面記事よ。

idioms.meaning: Taking great advantage of a situation, especially to criticize
idioms.meaning_ja: 大喜びしている、ここぞとばかりに楽しんでいる

idioms.meaning: Very important or sensational news
idioms.meaning_ja: 一面記事、トップニュース

dialogues.english: We're finally out of the woods. The hard part is over.
dialogues.japanese: やっと危機を脱した。大変な部分は終わったよ。

idioms.meaning: Past the most dangerous or difficult part
idioms.meaning_ja: 危機を脱した、最悪の時期を過ぎた

dialogues.english: Actions speak louder than words. Show them what you're made of, Johnny!
dialogues.japanese: 行動は言葉より雄弁よ。あなたの実力を見せてやりなさい、ジョニー！

idioms.meaning: What you do is more important than what you say
idioms.meaning_ja: 行動は言葉より雄弁、不言実行

idioms.meaning: One's true character or abilities
idioms.meaning_ja: あなたの本当の実力、何でできているか

dialogues.english: We might be underdogs, but that's what makes our story so special.
dialogues.japanese: 僕たちは弱者かもしれないけど、だからこそ僕たちの物語は特別なんだ。

idioms.meaning: People or teams expected to lose; the less favored competitor
idioms.meaning_ja: 弱者、不利な立場の人

dialogues.english: Playing for Clay was nerve-wracking, but I kept my cool.
dialogues.japanese: クレイの前で演奏するのはとても緊張したけど、冷静を保った。

idioms.meaning: Causing great anxiety or stress
idioms.meaning_ja: とても緊張する、ハラハラする

idioms.meaning: Stayed calm under pressure
idioms.meaning_ja: 冷静を保った、平常心を保った

dialogues.english: My heart was in my throat the whole performance. But I did it!
dialogues.japanese: パフォーマンス中ずっと心臓がドキドキだった。でもやったわ！

idioms.meaning: Extremely nervous or anxious
idioms.meaning_ja: 心臓が口から出そう、極度に緊張した

dialogues.english: When the crowd started cheering, I was over the moon. Pure joy.
dialogues.japanese: 観客が歓声を上げ始めた時、最高に嬉しかった。純粋な喜び。

idioms.meaning: Extremely happy and excited
idioms.meaning_ja: 大喜び、有頂天

dialogues.english: It was touch and go for a while. I honestly thought we wouldn't make it.
dialogues.japanese: しばらくは危なかったわ。正直、うまくいかないと思った。

idioms.meaning: A risky situation where the outcome is uncertain
idioms.meaning_ja: 危機一髪の、予断を許さない

idioms.meaning: To succeed or survive
idioms.meaning_ja: うまくいく、成功する

dialogues.english: Stepping on that stage again... it felt like a weight had been lifted off my shoulders.
dialogues.japanese: 再びあのステージに立つと...肩の荷が下りたような気がした。

idioms.expression: a weight lifted off my shoulders
idioms.meaning: A feeling of relief from worry or responsibility
idioms.meaning_ja: 肩の荷が下りた、重荷が取れた

dialogues.english: This whole adventure has been a roller coaster of emotions. But I wouldn't change a thing.
dialogues.japanese: この冒険は感情のジェットコースターだった。でも何一つ変えたくない。

idioms.meaning: An experience with many extreme emotional changes
idioms.meaning_ja: 感情のジェットコースター、激しい感情の起伏

idioms.meaning: Completely satisfied with how things turned out
idioms.meaning_ja: 何一つ変えたくない、すべてに満足

dialogues.english: Time is money, Moon. Stop beating around the bush and get to the point.
dialogues.japanese: 時は金なりだ、ムーン。回りくどいことはやめて本題に入れ。

idioms.meaning: Time is a valuable resource that should not be wasted
idioms.meaning_ja: 時は金なり

idioms.meaning: To state the main idea directly
idioms.meaning_ja: 本題に入る、要点を言う

dialogues.english: The reviews are in and they're raving about the show. Word of mouth is spreading like wildfire.
dialogues.japanese: レビューが出たわ。ショーを絶賛してる。口コミが瞬く間に広がってる。

idioms.meaning: Talking with great enthusiasm about something
idioms.meaning_ja: 絶賛している

idioms.meaning: Information passed from person to person by talking
idioms.meaning_ja: 口コミ

idioms.meaning: Spreading very quickly
idioms.meaning_ja: 瞬く間に広がる、あっという間に広まる

dialogues.english: We need to strike while the iron is hot. Let's book the next show immediately!
dialogues.japanese: 鉄は熱いうちに打たなきゃ。すぐに次のショーを予約しよう！

idioms.expression: strike while the iron is hot
idioms.meaning: To take advantage of an opportunity immediately
idioms.meaning_ja: 鉄は熱いうちに打て、好機を逃すな
